q: There are two types of capacitor combinations: parallel and series.
In a parallel combination, all the capacitors have the same ___.
In a parallel combination, do the capacitors carry the same or different amounts of charges?
The equivalent capacitance of capacitors connected in parallel is the sum of the capacitances of the individual capacitors.
For $n$ identical capacitors connected in parallel, the equivalent capacitance $C_p$ is ___.
In a series combination, the negatively charged plate of one capacitor is connected to the positively charged plate of the other.
Which property is the same for all capacitors connected in series?
Capacitors connected in series have different potential differences.
The total potential difference of a series combination is $V = V_1 + V_2 + ___$.
For capacitors in series, the reciprocal of the equivalent capacitance is the ___ of the reciprocals of their individual capacitances.
The equivalent capacitance $C_s$ of $n$ identical capacitors in series is $C_s = \frac{C}{n}$.
In series, the magnitude of charge on the equivalent capacitor is ___ when total potential difference $V$ is applied.
The equivalent capacitance of a series combination is always greater than any of the individual capacitances.
In which connection of the capacitors is the potential difference of each capacitor the same?
In series connection, each capacitor has the same charge.
